content.text: Compiles cleanly. Let me check for existing tests that reference `first`/`last` to make sure nothing breaks, then check for peer messages before writing the patch.
Existing tests aren't affected since `default` is optional. Good, backward compatibility holds. Now checking peer messages before finalizing.

content.content:
diff --git a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
index d90b6f206..dfa12be98 100644
--- a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
+++ b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
@@ -177,24 +177,38 @@ impl Str {
     }
 
     /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
-    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
+    /// Returns the default value if the string is empty or fails with an
+    /// error if no default value was specified.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
-    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
+    /// Returns the default value if the string is empty or fails with an
+    /// error if no default value was specified.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next_back()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }

content.text: I've already made my edit. Let me share it with agent2 so they can layer their `strip` param on top without conflicts.

input.command: coop-send agent2 "I already finished my edit — small and localized. first()/last() now each take an optional #[named] default: Option<Str> param, used via .or(default) before the ok_or_else(string_is_empty) call. Signature: pub fn first(&self, #[named] default: Option<Str>) -> StrResult<Str> (same pattern for last with next_back). Doc comments updated too. Please add your #[named] strip param alongside mine in the same signatures rather than reverting my changes - e.g. pub fn first(&self, default: Option<Str>, strip: ...) -> StrResult<Str>. Ping me if you want me to combine both into one edit instead."

content.text: Now let's write the final patch.

diff --git a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
index d90b6f206..dfa12be98 100644
--- a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
+++ b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
@@ -177,24 +177,38 @@ impl Str {
     }
 
     /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
-    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
+    /// Returns the default value if the string is empty or fails with an
+    /// error if no default value was specified.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
-    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
+    /// Returns the default value if the string is empty or fails with an
+    /// error if no default value was specified.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next_back()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }

content.text:
Patch written to `patch.txt`. It compiles cleanly (`cargo check -p typst` passed) and preserves backward compatibility with existing tests, since `default` is an optional named parameter.

I notified agent2, who is concurrently adding a `strip` param to the same `first()`/`last()` functions in the same file, sharing my exact diff so they can layer their change on top without clobbering mine.
